candidate: L4 CALENDAR/SEASONALITY scan -- turn-of-month, day-of-week, month-of-year, pre-holiday on XU100 daily returns. DESCRIPTIVE VIEW, multiple-testing-aware.
hypothesis: BIST exhibits documented calendar regularities (turn-of-month, day-of-week/Monday, January, pre-holiday). These are MARKET-WIDE timing effects, not cross-sectional stock signals. Tested as a descriptive scan with strict multiple-testing discipline.

data.market_series_PRIMARY: data/snapshots/exposure_d187_xu100.parquet (XU100 price index level, 2019-01-02..2026-04-29, investable market-timing proxy). Daily simple return.
data.market_series_SECONDARY: EW-full daily (honest bar, microcap-heavy, not directly investable) -- reported for robustness.

effects_FROZEN_NOT_SEARCHED.turn_of_month: effect-days = last trading day of month + first 3 trading days of next month; compare mean(effect) vs mean(other)
effects_FROZEN_NOT_SEARCHED.day_of_week: 5 tests: each weekday mean vs all-days (headline = Monday effect)
effects_FROZEN_NOT_SEARCHED.month_of_year: 12 month means (descriptive); headline = January (Jan vs rest)
effects_FROZEN_NOT_SEARCHED.pre_holiday: trading day immediately before a calendar gap >= 3 days to the next trading day (pre-long-weekend/holiday proxy) vs other days

multiple_testing.correction: Bonferroni on the 4-test headline family (alpha_adj = 0.05/4 = 0.0125) AND on the full 19-slice family (0.05/19 = 0.00263). Report RAW t/p, NW-t (daily autocorr), and survives-Bonferroni flags. No effect is claimed real unless it clears the corrected threshold.
multiple_testing.anti_phacking: The effect set is FIXED and canonical (declared here BEFORE measurement). NO data-driven search over windows/subperiods/sub-universes. This is the single most p-hacking-prone analysis -> maximal pre-registration discipline.

tradeability_frame.note: Each effect = a market-timing OVERLAY (hold the index only on effect-days). Harvesting requires frequent in/out trades AND sitting OUT of the market on non-effect-days. Under the strong positive inflation-drift of 2019-2026, being out of the market most days is a large opportunity-cost drag. The honest tradeable read = effect-day premium MINUS the non-effect-day return you forgo MINUS trade cost. Most calendar effects fail this even when statistically present.
tradeability_frame.metric: effect-day mean minus non-effect-day mean (differential); plus a hold-only-on-effect-days vs buy-and-hold annualized comparison (descriptive).

honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.summary: Calendar effects are the agent's lowest-priority cheap-scan: 'mostly not tradeable post-cost, document as VIEW.' Expect possibly a positive turn-of-month differential and weak day-of-week structure, BUT after multiple-testing correction and the out-of-market opportunity cost, NO deployable edge.
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.most_likely_verdict: DESCRIPTIVE-VIEW. Document any surviving-Bonferroni regularity as a market characteristic, NOT a tradeable edge. NO celebration; this closes the cheap-scan gap and is multiple-testing-honest.

keep_bar_FROZEN.definition: This is a VIEW by design. An effect is flagged NOTEWORTHY only if its differential clears the full-family Bonferroni threshold (p < 0.00263) AND NW-t supports it AND it is regime-sign-stable (2022-01 split). Even a noteworthy effect is labeled VIEW (not deployable) unless the hold-only-on-effect-days overlay beats buy-and-hold AFTER plausible index round-trip cost -- which is not expected.
